skill.name: pdf-ppt
skill.description: Create literature-report PPTX decks from PDF papers. Use when you must extract a paper's metadata, summarize the study, interpret Results/Figures/Tables, and generate slides with 1:1 figure-to-text alignment and layout rules (triggered by requests like "PDF to PPT", "literature report slides", or "turn this paper into a presentation").
skill.summary: Convert academic PDF papers into structured PPTX presentation slides with accurate figure-to-text mapping and professional layout.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 92 static analysis findings are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are triggered by hex color codes (#2C5F2D, #F96167, etc.) used for slide styling, not actual cryptography. The 'shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code formatting in documentation files. The 'system reconnaissance' findings are figure interpretation rules in academic context. The skill has a legitimate academic purpose (PDF to PPTX conversion) and passed a previous security audit with 'Production Ready' grade. The only genuine risk factor is external_commands, which is inherent to PDF processing using local tools like GROBID and PyMuPDF.

high_findings.title: Hex color codes misidentified as cryptographic material
high_findings.description: 24 HIGH-severity 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives. The scanner confused hex color codes (#2C5F2D, #F96167, #065A82, #990011) used for slide panel label styling with cryptographic hashes. Also triggered on YAML frontmatter and JSON audit file fields.

medium_findings.title: Markdown backtick formatting misidentified as shell execution
medium_findings.description: 66 MEDIUM-severity 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are false positives. All occurrences are in Markdown documentation files where backticks are used for code formatting, not shell execution. The skill appropriately documents PDF parsing workflows using tools like GROBID, PyMuPDF, and pdfplumber.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance misidentified in documentation
low_findings.description: 7 LOW-severity 'System reconnaissance' findings are false positives. Triggered by figure interpretation rules (e.g., 'Describe directionality explicitly') and audit JSON metadata fields. No reconnaissance commands present.

low_findings.title: Source URL misidentified as hardcoded network endpoint
low_findings.description: 2 LOW-severity 'Hardcoded URL' findings are false positives. The URL is a legitimate GitHub source attribution referencing the skill's origin repository. This is standard open-source practice and not a network call.

content.user_title: Turn PDF Research Papers into Presentation Slides
content.value_statement: Converting academic PDF papers into structured PPTX presentations is slow and error-prone. This skill automates the process by extracting paper metadata, figures, and tables, then generating professional literature-report slides with accurate figure-to-text alignment.
actual_capabilities: Extract paper metadata such as title, journal name, and publication date from PDF files
Parse and organize figure images with their corresponding legends and table content
Generate structured PPTX slides following standard academic presentation layouts
Produce Chinese-language figure interpretations with subfigure-level detail
Create figure-to-text mapping documentation in explication.json format
Apply consistent styling parameters including colors, fonts, and animations across all slides
limitations: Works best with text-based PDFs; scanned PDF documents require separate OCR preprocessing
Supplementary figures (Figure S1, S2) are ignored unless explicitly requested by the user
Output quality depends on the PDF file structure and metadata completeness
Requires local PDF parsing tools such as GROBID and PyMuPDF for offline execution

use_cases.title: Prepare Literature Review Slides for a Research Project
use_cases.description: A graduate student collects 10 PDF papers for their literature review. They use this skill to convert each paper into a structured PPTX deck with title slide, overview, figure-by-figure analysis, and summary slides.

use_cases.title: Create Weekly Lab Meeting Presentations
use_cases.description: A postdoctoral researcher needs to present a recent publication at lab meeting. They provide the PDF and receive a presentation with extracted figures, Chinese interpretation, and critical appraisal slides.

use_cases.title: Generate Seminar Slide Decks for Journal Club
use_cases.description: An academic librarian or journal club organizer converts a newly published paper into slides with proper figure-to-text alignment and standardized layout for group discussion.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PDF to PPT Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Please convert this PDF research paper into a literature-report PPTX with standard academic slide structure.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick conversion without special formatting requirements

prompt_templates.title: Conversion with Figure Interpretation
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this PDF into a literature-report PPTX. Interpret each figure and subfigure with purpose, method, object, and outcome. Use 1:1 figure-to-text alignment.
prompt_templates.scenario: When detailed figure-by-figure analysis is needed

prompt_templates.title: Conversion with Custom Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this paper into a presentation. Follow the v5 layout rules with title box at (2.0825, 1.796, 9.1683, 1.2) and 32pt bold centered text for the title slide.
prompt_templates.scenario: When specific slide layout coordinates are required

prompt_templates.title: Full Customization with Chinese Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Please convert this paper PDF into a literature-report PPT. Requirements: interpret each figure in Chinese, keep figure-to-text alignment, follow v5 layout rules, and include a critical appraisal on the final slide.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete workflow with Chinese language output and critical analysis

output_examples.input: Please convert this lung cancer research PDF into a literature-report PPTX with figure interpretation in Chinese.
output_examples.output: Generated PPTX with 8 slides: (1) Title slide showing paper title, journal, and publication date; (2) Overview slide with background and key findings in Chinese; (3-6) Four figure slides each with extracted image on right and Chinese interpretation text on left; (7-8) Summary and critical appraisal slides with core highlights and limitations.

output_examples.input: Convert this PDF to PPT and make sure each figure has its own slide with 1-to-1 alignment.
output_examples.output: PPTX with 6 slides where each figure slide contains exactly one extracted figure image positioned on the right side and corresponding interpretation text on the left side. Figure labels are bolded and panel label color is consistently applied across the deck.

best_practices: Always verify that extracted figure images match their corresponding legends before generating the final presentation
Review the Chinese interpretation text for technical accuracy and appropriate academic tone before sharing
Provide a figure_titles_zh.txt mapping file when you have pre-existing Chinese translations for figure titles
anti_patterns: Requesting conversion of supplementary materials such as Figure S1 and S2 without explicit instruction to include them
Expecting the skill to call external APIs for PDF parsing when the workflow is designed as offline-first
Relying on the skill to interpret figures without providing access to the full Results section text

faq.question: What PDF formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill works with standard text-based academic PDF files. Scanned image-only PDFs require preprocessing with OCR tools before this skill can process them.

faq.question: Does this skill require internet access?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for offline-first execution. It uses local tools such as GROBID and PyMuPDF for PDF parsing and does not call external APIs.

faq.question: Can the skill handle scanned PDF documents?
faq.answer: Scanned PDFs require OCR preprocessing using tools like OCRmyPDF and Tesseract before the skill can extract text and figures. This step must be done separately.

faq.question: What languages does the slide output support?
faq.answer: The skill is designed to produce figure interpretations in professional Chinese. Slide titles and metadata can include both English and Chinese content.

faq.question: How are figure and table slides organized?
faq.answer: Each figure receives its own slide with the extracted image on one side and corresponding interpretation text on the other. Figures appear in the same order as the Results section.

faq.question: Can I customize the slide layout and styling?
faq.answer: Yes. You can provide a custom layout script with specific coordinates, font sizes, colors, and animation presets. The skill will follow these parameters across the entire deck.
